title: The bench with a name
summary: In a Madrid park, one bench has a small metal plaque. People notice it without knowing why.
body:
In a small park in Madrid, between two trees, there is a bench. It is the same colour as the others, dark green. But on the back, there is a small metal plaque, the size of a phone.

It says: 'For Pilar, who liked this view. 1939 to 2014.'

The park is not famous. Old people sit there in the morning, children play in the afternoon. Nobody talks about the plaque. But people notice it the way you notice a quiet song in a noisy room.

Last spring, a man started to clean the plaque every Sunday. He brought a small cloth and a little bottle of water. He worked for two minutes, then sat for a few minutes, then walked away. He did not say anything to anybody. He was about sixty.

A neighbour, an older woman who walks her dog there, asked another, 'Is that her son?' The other neighbour said, 'I think so. Or her brother.'

This Sunday the man came as usual. But this time, a young girl, maybe ten years old, was sitting on the bench. She did not move. The man stopped. He looked at her. She looked at him.

He smiled. He said quietly, 'It is a good view, no?'

The girl said, 'Yes.'

The man cleaned the plaque around her shoulder. He did not ask her to move. He did not explain. When he finished, he sat on the grass nearby. The girl stayed. Two strangers, one bench, one name.

annotations.phrase: a quiet song in a noisy room
annotations.meaningVi: một bài hát êm trong căn phòng ồn — phép so sánh: thứ nhỏ vẫn được nhận ra

annotations.phrase: two strangers, one bench, one name
annotations.meaningVi: hai người lạ, một ghế băng, một cái tên — câu kết kiểu liệt kê ngắn

grammarNotes.title: Past simple negative with 'did not'
grammarNotes.bodyVi: A2 dùng 'did not + V (nguyên thể)' cho phủ định ở quá khứ đơn. Trong bài: 'He did not say anything to anybody.' 'She did not move.' 'He did not ask her to move. He did not explain.' Sau 'did not' luôn dùng động từ nguyên thể (move, say, ask, explain), KHÔNG thêm '-ed'. 'Did not' thường viết tắt là 'didn't' trong văn nói.
grammarNotes.bodyEn: A2 forms past-simple negatives with 'did not + base verb.' From the passage: 'He did not say anything to anybody.' 'She did not move.' 'He did not ask her to move. He did not explain.' After 'did not' the verb stays in its base form (move, say, ask, explain) — never '-ed.' 'Did not' usually contracts to 'didn't' in speech.

translationVi:
Trong một công viên nhỏ ở Madrid, giữa hai cái cây, có một chiếc ghế băng. Ghế đã cũ. Nó cùng màu với những chiếc ghế khác trong công viên, xanh đậm. Nhưng phía sau ghế này, có một tấm biển kim loại nhỏ. Tấm biển to bằng cái điện thoại.

Nó viết: 'Tặng Pilar, người yêu cảnh nhìn này. 1939 đến 2014.'

Công viên không nổi tiếng. Phần lớn người già ngồi đó vào buổi sáng, trẻ con chơi vào buổi chiều. Không ai nói về tấm biển. Nhưng người ta để ý. Họ để ý theo cách bạn để ý một bài hát êm trong căn phòng ồn.

Mùa xuân vừa rồi, một người đàn ông bắt đầu lau tấm biển mỗi Chủ Nhật. Ông mang một mảnh vải nhỏ và một chai nước nhỏ. Ông làm trong hai phút, rồi ngồi vài phút, rồi đi. Ông không nói gì với ai. Ông tầm sáu mươi tuổi.

Một người hàng xóm, một phụ nữ lớn tuổi dắt chó đi dạo ở đó, hỏi một hàng xóm khác: 'Có phải con trai bà ấy không?' Người kia nói: 'Tôi nghĩ vậy. Hoặc là em trai. Tôi không biết.'

Chủ Nhật này người đàn ông đến như thường. Nhưng lần này, một cô bé chừng mười tuổi đang ngồi trên ghế. Cô bé không nhúc nhích. Người đàn ông dừng lại. Ông nhìn cô bé. Cô bé nhìn ông.

Ông mỉm cười. Ông nói khẽ: 'Cảnh đẹp, đúng không?'

Cô bé nói: 'Vâng.'

Ông lau tấm biển quanh vai cô bé. Ông không bảo cô bé dời chỗ. Ông không giải thích. Khi xong, ông ngồi xuống bãi cỏ gần đó. Cô bé vẫn ngồi đó. Hai người lạ, một ghế băng, một cái tên.

questions.prompt: What is special about this bench in the park?
options: It has a small metal plaque on the back.
It is a different colour from the others.
It is much bigger than the others.
questions.explanation: 'On the back of this bench, there is a small metal plaque.'

options: 'For Pilar, who liked this view. 1939 to 2014.'

questions.explanation: 'The plaque is the size of a phone.'

options: Old people in the morning, children in the afternoon.
questions.hint: Paragraph about who uses the park.

questions.prompt: What does the man do every Sunday?
options: He cleans the plaque with a cloth and a little water.
He sits and reads a book.

questions.prompt: How long does the cleaning take?
What do the neighbours think about him?
options: He may be Pilar's son or brother — they are not sure.
He is a stranger from another city.
questions.explanation: 'Is that her son?' 'I think so. Or her brother.'

options: A young girl is sitting on the bench and does not move.
questions.explanation: 'A young girl, maybe ten years old, was sitting on the bench. She did not move.'

questions.prompt: What does the man do when he sees the girl on the bench?
options: He smiles, says 'It is a good view, no?', and cleans the plaque around her shoulder.
A small act of weekly care for a stranger's plaque is shared, without explanation, with a child who happens to be there.
Parks should put more plaques on their benches.
Old people should not sit on benches in the afternoon.
Cleaning is a job for the city government.
questions.explanation: The closing scene and the man's quiet acceptance of the girl.

questions.prompt: What does 'the way you notice a quiet song in a noisy room' mean here?
options: People notice the plaque even though nobody talks about it.
questions.explanation: A simile for small things noticed without being discussed.

The park is not famous. Old people sit there in the morning, children play in the afternoon. Nobody talks about the plaque. But people notice it the way you notice a quiet song in a noisy {{b4}}.

A neighbour, an older woman who walks her {{b6}} there, asked another, 'Is that her son?' The other neighbour said, 'I think so. Or her brother.'

This Sunday the man came as usual. But this time, a young girl, maybe ten years old, was {{b7}} on the bench. She did not move. The man stopped. He looked at her. She looked at him.

blanks.explanation: A quiet song in a noisy room.

criticalThinkingQuestion: The man cleans the plaque around the girl's shoulder rather than asking her to move. What does this small choice say about how he understands the bench's purpose — and would you have made the same choice?
